title: My Morning Routine
story_html: Every day, I get up at seven o'clock. Then I eat breakfast with my family. My mom cooks a special egg for me. My dad buys sweet mangoes at the market. After school, my classmate comes to my house. We share snacks together. He is generous and gives me extra chips. Then we do homework. I practice counting numbers for math. My pet is a small kangaroo. It has soft fur. I pet it every day.

special.def: Different from usual, in a good way.

snacks.def: Small amounts of food eaten between meals.

homework.def: School work that students do at home.

counting.def: Saying numbers in order to find a total.

generous.def: Willing to give more than necessary.

fur.def: The soft hair that covers some animals.

classmate.def: A student who is in the same class as you.

eat breakfast.def: To eat the first meal of the day.

kangaroo.def: An animal that jumps and carries its baby in a pouch.

questions.qtext: "He is generous and gives me extra chips." What does generous mean?
questions.explain: Generous means willing to give more than necessary.

questions.qtext: "My pet is a small kangaroo. It has soft fur." What is fur?
options: The soft hair on an animal
questions.explain: Fur is the soft hair that covers some animals.

questions.qtext: What time does the child get up?
questions.explain: The story says, "I get up at seven o'clock."

questions.qtext: What does the dad buy at the market?
questions.explain: The story says, "My dad buys sweet mangoes at the market."

questions.qtext: Who comes to the child's house after school?
questions.explain: The story says, "my classmate comes to my house."
